title: Caducus CLI: Collect and analyze ops events

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: MVP scope: Single coherent CLI that (1) collects events via pluggable collectors - thin layer over Virtuus, writes canonical JSON schema to folder; (2) runs analysis via Biblicus (thin wrapper). Built-in collectors: CloudWatch Logs, SQS DLQ, one alert channel. Pluggable collector protocol for extensions. Virtuus schema: events table with GSIs. group_id strategy: configurable per collector, default from source + logical identifier (log group, queue, channel).

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
**Configuration design (dotyaml pattern):**

- Hierarchy: YAML (merged) → .env → env vars → CLI `--config` overrides
- Config paths: `~/.caducus/config.yml`, `./.caducus/config.yml`, optional `--configuration` explicit path(s)
- Single config file contains: Caducus keys (data_dir, collectors, etc.) + pass-through `biblicus` section
- No schema duplication: `biblicus` subtree uses Biblicus's own structure, passed through to Biblicus when Caducus invokes analysis
- Env vars: Biblicus's existing vars (OPENAI_API_KEY, BIBLICUS_*, etc.) work as-is; YAML can interpolate `{{ VAR|default }}`
- CLI: one `--config key=value` (dotted) for any path, e.g. `--config biblicus.reinforcement_memory.embed.model=other`
- Either Biblicus adds `from_config`, or Caducus does thin adapter from `biblicus` dict to ReinforcementMemory constructor
